record.field: Apache Superset is a modern, enterprise-ready data exploration and visualization platform that turns any SQL data source into interactive dashboards.

Apache Superset is a modern, enterprise-ready data exploration and visualization platform that turns any SQL data source into interactive dashboards.

Maintained by the Apache Software Foundation, Superset lets analysts and data teams build charts and dashboards with no code while also offering a full SQL editor for power users. It connects to virtually any SQL-speaking database or data engine through SQLAlchemy and is proven at massive scale by companies such as Airbnb, Dropbox and Netflix, making it a credible open-source alternative to proprietary BI suites.

Key features
  • No-code chart builder with 40+ visualization types and a plugin architecture for custom charts
  • SQL Lab IDE for ad-hoc queries, metadata browsing and Jinja templating
  • Connects to 40+ databases and data engines (PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, Snowflake, Trino, Presto and more)
  • Interactive dashboards with cross-filters, drill-down and a reusable semantic layer of datasets and metrics
  • Configurable caching layer plus async queries for performance at scale
  • Granular role-based access control, multiple authentication backends and a REST API
Best for

Data teams, analysts and organizations that want a self-hosted, open-source business intelligence and dashboarding platform to explore SQL data and share insights without per-seat licensing.

services: superset: image: amancevice/superset:6.0.0 restart: unless-stopped depends_on: - superset_postgres - superset_redis volumes: - ../files/superset/superset_config.py:/etc/superset/superset_config.py environment: SECRET_KEY: {{ secret_key }} MAPBOX_API_KEY: {{ mapbox_api_key }} POSTGRES_USER: {{ postgres_user }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} POSTGRES_DB: {{ postgres_db }} REDIS_PASSWORD: {{ redis_password }} POSTGRES_HOST: superset_postgres REDIS_HOST: superset_redis container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8088' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network superset_postgres: image: postgres:18 restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_USER: {{ postgres_user }} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: {{ postgres_password }} POSTGRES_DB: {{ postgres_db }} volumes: - ./superset_postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U ${{ postgres_user }} -d ${{ postgres_db }} interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_superset_postgres' networks: - internal superset_redis: image: redis:8 restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./superset_redis_data:/data command: redis-server --requirepass {{ redis_password }} healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - redis-cli -a ${{ redis_password }} ping interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_superset_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T superset_postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d superset_postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T superset_postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T superset_postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T superset_postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0

""" For more configuration options, see: - https://superset.apache.org/docs/configuration/configuring-superset """
import os
SECRET_KEY = os.getenv("SECRET_KEY") MAPBOX_API_KEY = os.getenv("MAPBOX_API_KEY", "")
CACHE_CONFIG = { "CACHE_TYPE": "RedisCache", "CACHE_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT": 300, "CACHE_KEY_PREFIX": "superset_", "CACHE_REDIS_HOST": "redis", "CACHE_REDIS_PORT": 6379, "CACHE_REDIS_DB": 1, "CACHE_REDIS_URL": f"redis://:{os.getenv('REDIS_PASSWORD')}@{os.getenv('REDIS_HOST')}:6379/1", }
FILTER_STATE_CACHE_CONFIG = {**CACHE_CONFIG, "CACHE_KEY_PREFIX": "superset_filter_"} EXPLORE_FORM_DATA_CACHE_CONFIG = {**CACHE_CONFIG, "CACHE_KEY_PREFIX": "superset_explore_form_"}
SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS = True SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = f"postgresql+psycopg2://{os.getenv('POSTGRES_USER')}:{os.getenv('POSTGRES_PASSWORD')}@{os.getenv('POSTGRES_HOST')}:5432/{os.getenv('POSTGRES_DB')}"
# Uncomment if you want to load example data (using "superset load_examples") at the # same location as your metadata postgresql instance. Otherwise, the default sqlite # will be used, which will not persist in volume when restarting superset by default. #SQLALCHEMY_EXAMPLES_URI = SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI
